mnemonics: THIS Means 'This Instance's Something' - To distinguish between instance variables and parameters
keyPoints: 'this' is a reference to the current object whose method is being called.
It is primarily used to resolve ambiguity between instance variables and parameters.
It can be used to call one constructor from another constructor in the same class (constructor chaining).
commonMistakes: Using 'this' unnecessarily when there is no naming conflict, which adds visual clutter.
Attempting to use 'this' in a static context (static methods), where there is no current object.
Forgetting to use 'this' when an instance variable is shadowed by a method parameter, leading to accidental assignment of a parameter to itself.
